excerpt: By January 1957, the terrorist organization in Algiers comprised approximately 1200 armed men (ALN) and 4500 persons belonging to the FLN.
transform_note: Used as the insurgent armed-personnel input for military person-months; broader FLN political apparatus not counted in core denominators.
confidence_note: Strong within-source distinction between armed ALN and broader FLN network.

excerpt: Opposing this formidable force were scarcely 1000 municipal police.
transform_note: Included only in the best military-denominator scenario because their exact direct-combat role after army takeover is less precisely enumerated than the paras.
confidence_note: Useful lower-mid French organized-armed benchmark for the urban phase.

excerpt: The battle began in January 1957 when, following a series of bomb attacks, 4,600 paratroopers were deployed onto the streets.
transform_note: Used as the low French-force input and as the anchor for the army-takeover date.
confidence_note: Strong scholarly synthesis but still a rounded deployment figure.

excerpt: Le général Massu dispose dans le secteur Alger-Sahel ... de 10 000 soldats, dont quatre régiments de sa division parachutiste.
transform_note: Used only as the high military-denominator ceiling because the sector Alger-Sahel is broader than the central urban core.
confidence_note: High-value ceiling, but geography is wider than the civilian denominator.

excerpt: Avec 365 040 habitants en 1954, Alger était la 4e ville française derrière Paris, Marseille et Lyon.
transform_note: Used as the low civilian-denominator bound for the Jan-Oct 1957 episode.
confidence_note: Tertiary restatement of census-era data; acceptable only as a bound.

excerpt: Avec sa banlieue ... la population totale de l'agglomération s'élevait en 1954 à 580 086 habitants.
transform_note: Used as the best civilian-denominator bound because bombings, strike-breaking, and security operations extended beyond the commune core.
confidence_note: Tertiary restatement, but conceptually better matched to the urban campaign than the commune alone.

transform_note: Used as the high civilian-denominator bound.
confidence_note: Strongest official demographic input in the bundle.

excerpt: Yacef Saadi avait perdu moins d'un millier d'hommes — très probablement ... trois cents tués.
transform_note: Retained as context only because the figure concerns losses in the ZAA/terrorist organization, not an explicit combatant-only death series.
confidence_note: Important but status-contaminated numerator; unusable for a strict mil_deaths_direct estimate.

excerpt: la bataille d'Alger s'acheva par une défaite du terrorisme, qui aurait commis en quatorze mois 751 attentats, et causé 314 morts et 917 blessés.
transform_note: Broader than ALGW-E4 and mixed-status; not allocated to civilians or organized armed personnel in core estimates.
confidence_note: Useful attack-victim context but period and status scope do not match core DR requirements.

transform_note: Stored as auxiliary injury context only.

geographic_scope: Grand Alger / Battle of Algiers context
excerpt: « 3 024 disparus de la Bataille d’Alger » — évalués ainsi de manière très hypothétique par Paul Teitgen.
transform_note: Explicitly not converted into direct deaths in core estimates.
confidence_note: Historically central but hypothesis-labeled disappearance figure.

excerpt: publication d’une liste de 1010 de leurs noms
pages_or_sections: main text, line 167 and note [65] line 285
transform_note: Named-case floor for disappearance research, not a direct-death count.
confidence_note: Important named-case evidence that still does not resolve explicit death status.
